items.summary: World number three Novak Djokovic falls to defending champion Jack Draper in a pulsating fourth-round match at the prestigious Indian Wells tournament.

items.title: Albanese government won't say the 'w' word about Iran
items.summary: This week in Canberra the Nationals chose a new leader, the energy minister chose his words carefully and the government chose to avoid one word altogether.

items.title: Wife of Australian footballer Barry Cable takes stand in his child sex abuse trial
items.summary: The wife of Barry Cable, the former footballer on trial for allegedly sexually abusing a child in the early stages of his illustrious career, testifies in defence of her famous husband.

items.title: Tigers hopeful Heamasi Makasini stays with NRL club amid talk of rugby union switch
items.summary: Heamasi Makasini has shown off his talent in both rugby codes, but Wests Tigers are hopeful they will keep the 18-year-old at the club once his contract expires.

items.title: Fuel standards relaxed to add 100 million litres of fuel to Australian supply
items.summary: Australia will bring in 100 million more litres of petrol by allowing a higher level of sulfur content in its fuel for the next 60 days, Energy Minister Chris Bowen says, as the country reckons with petrol flows choked by the Middle East war.

items.title: Oil price breaks the $US100 per barrel mark as oil tankers are hit in the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The oil price has surged again amid Iranian strikes on oil tankers and the fear there could be more attacks to come.

items.title: SA Liberal candidate no longer running for party after podcast comments
items.summary: SA Liberal Leader Ashton Hurn says one of her candidates for the upcoming state election is no longer running for the party after comments he made on Islam, feminism, same-sex marriage and "the trans agenda" came to light from an American podcast.

items.title: Boil water alert lifted in Darwin after testing shows region's water is free of E.coli and other 'harmful bacteria'
items.summary: The boil water alert for Darwin has been lifted after testing confirmed the region's water was free of E.coli and other harmful bacteria.

items.title: Deeper oil crisis could add full percentage point to inflation, Treasury warns
items.summary: Treasury has advised the government that if the oil price averages $US100 per barrel for three months, there would be a temporary 0.5 percentage point spike to headline inflation.

items.title: Atlassian cuts 1,600 jobs from global workforce as AI slashes labour needs
items.summary: Atlassian's Australian workforce will account for 30 per cent of the tech giant's global job cuts, with the freed-up funds to be invested in AI and enterprise sales.

items.title: Country councils putting compulsory voting for WA's local government elections back on agenda
items.summary: Councillors in one of WA's biggest regional cities back making voting at local government elections compulsory, amid growing concerns about low turnout and fringe candidates running for councils across the state.

items.title: Mother jailed for criminal neglect of three children, including 6yo Charlie, who died
items.summary: A mother who admitted to criminally neglecting three of her children, including her daughter who died when she was six-years-old, has been jailed for 17 years.

items.title: Beth Mooney sold for record fee in Women's Hundred auction
items.summary: Australia's evergreen run machine Beth Mooney has cashed in on her enduring excellence, scooping a joint-record payday of $395,000 in the auction for The Women's Hundred.

items.title: Man found wounded at supermarket assisting police as woman and child found dead
items.summary: After speaking with a wounded man at a supermarket in Logan about 7:30 this morning, police attended a nearby address and found the bodies of a woman and child.

items.title: NSW defeat Tasmania by four wickets to win One Day Cup final
items.summary: A steady hand from skipper Kurtis Patterson, after early damage by Sean Abbott and Ben Dwarshuis, has helped NSW to a One Day Cup title with a rain-hit four-wicket win over Tasmania.

items.title: Islamic Society criticises 'competition' to attack Muslims as SA Liberals stand by candidate
items.summary: An organisation representing South Australian Muslims criticises the Liberal Party and One Nation for what it says is a rush to attack Islam "more harshly in order to gain political support" ahead of the state election.

items.title: Cambodia says it will shut down online scam centres by the end of April
items.summary: Cambodian authorities say they have shut down 80 per cent of locations believed to be carrying out online scams. But some are sceptical given "key perpetrators" among the country's ruling elite.

items.title: Targeting mistake led to US missile strike on Iranian girls school: media reports
items.summary: A US military investigation has determined that a US Tomahawk missile struck an Iranian elementary school because of a targeting mistake, The New York Times has reported.

items.title: After watching all 211 of Alex Johnston's NRL tries, these five are the most important

items.title: Former ASIO boss Dennis Richardson says he was 'surplus to requirements' of royal commission
items.summary: Former ASIO boss Dennis Richardson says he resigned from the royal commission into antisemitism because he felt he was overpaid and "surplus to requirements".

items.title: Australia's summers are getting longer with more extreme heat
items.summary: Australia has always been hot. On average, summer is now one degree warmer than it was 50 years ago. But that number hides the stark reality that the summers our kids experience today are much more extreme and longer than they used to be.

items.title: After 40 years of parenthood incentives Singapore's fertility rate is still falling

items.title: Why IV drips are surging in popularity in Australia's wellness space
items.summary: Some advocates say vitamin IV drips can promote clearer thinking, immunity, beauty and even anti-aging, but health experts say there is little research to back the claims.

items.summary: Two top doctors and a nurse have left Albury Wodonga hospital in just six months, prompting a vote of no confidence in management.

items.title: North Queensland residents say electoral boundary changes leave them 'short-changed'
items.summary: With one parliamentarian from the far north set to go, residents in some areas will go from being 15 minutes' drive away from their local MP to 15 hours.

items.summary: SA Police officers continue their resumed search for missing four-year-old Gus Lamont after returning to Oak Park Station in Yunta.

items.title: Sydney's abnormal heat, humidity levels due to warm Tasman Sea and northerly winds
items.summary: Sydney's humidity has been rivalling the tropics in the middle of wet season, with the stifling conditions expected to linger into autumn.

items.title: German Paralympic Games protest of Russian gold medallist under investigation
items.summary: Germany's Paralympians stage their own protest against Russia's inclusion at the Milano Cortina Games, while Ukraine says its athletes have been mistreated by the International Paralympic ‌Committee.

items.title: ABC Oscars quiz: How well do you remember the winners and losers from the past 10 years?
items.summary: Test your knowledge of who went home with a statue and who was honoured just to be nominated.

items.summary: Residents in the Adelaide Hills town of Kanmantoo and surrounds have been dealing with a growing regularity of pungent odour but the fertiliser company, who pleaded guilty to environmental breaches, says its chicken litter presents no risk.

items.summary: Kalyn Ponga says previous appearances for Australia did not reflect his true national identity after the International Rugby League approved his application to switch allegiances to New Zealand.

items.title: Lauren Parker finishes eighth in women's 10km sitting para cross-country skiing, Oksana Masters wins gold
items.summary: Lauren Parker has finished eighth in the women's 10km sitting para cross-country skiing at the Milano Cortina Games, just nine months after taking up the sport.

items.title: More fuel tankers struck as crew members trapped on ship in Strait of Hormuz — as it happened
items.summary: Multiple ships have been targeted in the Gulf, including two tankers that have been set ablaze in Iraqi waters after strikes by Iranian boats.

items.title: Iconic Australian-made pinball machines preserved for generations to come
items.summary: Five pinball machines, including the first licensed Star Wars unit, have been saved from the scrap heap and will eventually be made available for the public to play at a special exhibition.

items.title: Kharg Island could be Iran's weak spot as Washington reportedly considers ground invasion options
items.summary: There is a small island in the Persian Gulf that is vital to the flow of Iranian oil to its customers and, analysts say, if Washington or Israel were to seize it the regime's war chest would soon dry up.

items.title: Record 400 million barrels of oil to be released from reserves to stall price hike
items.summary: The International Energy Agency agrees to release 400 million barrels of oil, the largest such move in its history. Australia says it is considering the decision, which would not involve sending oil overseas.

items.title: Approval granted for major expansion of Porters Bridge Road Quarry despite concerns
items.summary: Locals say the "continuous rumbling" from a quarry in Tasmania's Meander Valley is a major disturbance. The company which owns it has just been given approval to increase production by 500 per cent.

items.title: Victorian wineries sell vineyards to gold exploration company amid global headwinds
items.summary: As the wine industry struggles with increased challenges, some Victorian wineries are choosing to sell their land to mining companies.

items.title: The real-life story behind The Voice of Hind Rajab
items.summary: Protests erupted following the killing of six-year-old Hind Rajab at the hands of Israeli forces. Two years later, her story is in the running for an Academy Award.

items.title: Victorian government vows to shift building inspection costs from buyers to sellers
items.summary: The Victorian government says it will make home sellers pay for building and pest inspections rather than home buyers if it wins this year's state election in November.

items.title: Matt Canavan may blunt One Nation but can he help rebuild the Coalition?
items.summary: Matt Canavan's rise to the Nationals leadership gives the Coalition a fighter capable of taking on Pauline Hanson, but beating back One Nation is only part of the opposition's electoral dilemma.

items.title: Former spy boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission
items.summary: Dennis Richardson was originally tapped by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in the weeks after the Bondi terror attack to lead a review into the federal government's intelligence and security agencies.

items.title: Bird deaths linked to fish lost in algal bloom, SA Research and Development Institute says
items.summary: Birds are starving and dying along metropolitan Adelaide beaches, caused by a lack of fish as a result of the algal bloom, the South Australian government says.

items.title: Tasmania Police officer and a Franklin River blockade photographer reunite after four decades
items.summary: Ian Wright captured the Franklin River blockade on film. Forty years later, the officer who arrested him reached out.

items.title: Teenage pair rack up charges during alleged cross-state crime spree
items.summary: A Mildura man has been remanded in custody and his accomplice remains on the run after the pair allegedly went on a four-day crime spree across Victoria.

items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Flower importers feel the pinch as Iran war affects flight routes
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is not just affecting petrol prices and travel plans — florists and their suppliers have suddenly found themselves unable to supply blooms due to the disruption of air freight routes.

items.title: How the Middle East war and the cost of fuel is sending airfares skyward
items.summary: As Qantas hikes international airfares due to a surge in fuel prices from the Middle East war, an industry expert warns it's only the start of what's to come.

items.title: Mother of Iranian women's footballer pleads with player to stay in Australia as families face threats
items.summary: The smiles and joy seen in pictures of the seven members of the Iranian women's football team who sought asylum in Australia only tell part of the story, with many still scared for their families back home.

items.title: Economists scramble to change their interest rate forecasts after RBA speaks
items.summary: Expectations the Reserve Bank will hike the cash rate next week are rising as leading economists shift their forecasts — and some say one increase may not be enough.

items.title: The Tasmania Devils won't play AFL at Macquarie Point earlier than 2031. Should we be surprised?
items.summary: Tasmania's future AFL club believed they would be playing in their new $1.13-billion stadium from 2030, but it's now clear their debut there will be at least one year later.

items.title: Women tell court Barry Cable sexually assaulted them as children in Melbourne backyard spas
items.summary: Former footballer Barry Cable sexually assaulted children in a backyard spa in Melbourne at a time when he was a "famous" figure, a court has heard.
